# Cutbykeenan — Profile

_Last updated 2026-05-19 from a 22-video CIS audit corpus (n=22). All metrics, archetypes, and prescription lanes below are derived from that corpus; the operator (Brad / Dack) confirms before any change is taken as proven._

## Identity

- **Handle (Instagram):** @cutbykeenan
- **Uploader display name:** "MACUNGIE BARBER • KEENAN" (carried on every post)
- **Handle (TikTok):** TBD — Dack confirm
- **Handle (YouTube):** TBD — Dack confirm
- **Real name (optional):** Keenan
- **Location:** Macungie / Allentown PA — Lehigh Valley (confirmed: caption hashtags `#macungie #allentown #PA #lehighvalley`-intended on multiple posts, plus the uploader name)
- **Languages:** English (no Spanish or other captions in any of the 22 posts)

## Niche & Specialty

- **Primary niche:** Youth / kids' / young-adult barbering — solo in-home setup
- **Sub-specialties (visible across the 22-video corpus):**
  - **Textured scissor crops** with low / drop / burst tapers (most common — appears 12+ times)
  - **Korean-style textured bowl crops** with drop fade
  - **Curly / natural-curl crops** with low tapers (including bleached / highlight tips work — striking cuts)
  - **High-top afros with hard-part line work** + clean fades + chin strap
  - **Disconnected textured + low taper** (longer-fringe variants)
- **Tools/products they're known for:** Wahl clipper (visible in captions as `#wahl`). Styling spray seen once (DJkybfjpIQ4). No identifiable signature product line.

## Archetype

- **Content archetype:** **Silent finished-cut community-flex showcaser** (current; 19/22 posts) — pivoting toward authority_demonstration (1 multi-cut compilation, v15) and community_flex / before_after (1 attempt, v17).
- **Voice / personality:** Visual-only — barber does not appear or speak in 21/22 posts (lone exception: v15 selfie open). Personality is delivered through cut quality and (rare) cover/face-visible angle choices.
- **Tone defaults:** Calm demonstration, music-bed only. Music choice trends toward explicit rap (≥10 posts); occasional R&B / soft songs.
- **Tier:** Solo home-barber in build phase — book-building stage, technical craft already solid.

## Audience

- **Estimated demo:** Lehigh Valley youth + young-male client base (visible in client mix: kids, teens, young adults). Engagement community is small + loyal (recurring commenter handles `seasidegsl55`, `rahil_parm`, `geofadeit`, `jgtm223`).
- **Follower count (snapshot date — YYYY-MM-DD):** TBD — not exposed in the post metadata captured. Dack confirm.
- **Typical engagement-per-view baseline:** Views are hidden in IG metadata (-1 for all 22 posts). Likes range **12–39** across the corpus, median **~20 likes / 1 comment**. Cap appears engagement-driven, not reach-driven (small consistent audience, plateaued).
- **What they comment most:** Fire-emoji praise (`🔥🔥🔥`), short fan praise ("hell yeahh keenan", "great cut Keenan", "go white boy go"). One external-barber DM request ("Send me post ples"). No format-debate or judgment comments.

## Performance baseline

- **Average likes per post (22-video corpus):** ~20 likes (mean), median 19, range 12–39
- **Average comments per post:** ~1.4 (range 0–5; 6/22 posts landed 0 comments)
- **Top-performing format historically:** **Multi-cut compilation with frame-1 on-screen text + barber-selfie open** — n=1 (DOzizVYCZgB, 39 likes, +30% over the prior best). This is the corpus's single largest engagement lift and the only post that combined the missing on-screen-text lever with a format pivot.
- **Second-best format:** Silent single-client showcase with a striking cut + face-visible side-profile cover — proven n=2 (DNYJPVtO7Wi 30 likes, DPq_0xUjko2 28 likes). Confirms the silent-template ceiling at 28–30 when execution discipline is right.
- **Worst-performing format historically:** Silent showcase with back-of-head opener / cover OR caption-video transformation mismatch (DHeLlKspg2q 14/3, DM6eF-bJZHG 18/0, DNo7iJ6O_eY 12/0, DQmYvxikf6h 14/0).
- **Last update:** 2026-05-19 (n=22 corpus encoding by CIS, operator-approved).

## Strengths

- **Technical scissor + taper craft across cut types** — textured crops, bowls, drop fades, high-tops with hard parts, curly highlight work all execute cleanly. Skill is genuinely good; the ceiling is not craft.
- **Range of cut types** — 22 posts cover 5+ distinct cut families (textured crops, bowls, drop fades, high-tops, curly/highlight) with a different client almost every post. Demonstrates flexibility and a recurring client base.
- **Booking infrastructure already exists** — caption arc went from no offer → "tap in for a free cut" → "dm me for a free cut" → "$10 cuts" → "Book with me, booking page in my bio". The business pitch is maturing (just stays caption-only).
- **One real format breakthrough already on record** — v15 (DOzizVYCZgB) proves the on-screen-text + compilation lane works for him; the formula is documented, just under-used (used 1/22).

## Weaknesses / Coaching Areas

- **0 on-screen text on 20/22 posts** — the single dominant un-pulled lever in the corpus. v15 is the lone proof and the corpus high (39); v17 second proof at 16 (weak before contrast killed it). Every caption-only offer (free cut → $10 → booking page) stays buried because the video layer never reinforces it.
- **Recurring back-of-head cover habit (5+ posts)** — covers are made-of-frame choice, not a craft problem; this is the lowest-effort fix.
- **Runtime creep on multiple posts** — 3 posts at 15–16s (DIM1YzLJxuf, DIuZh7HpXlS, DOwxt86CTiY) all engagement-cap; the payoff is done by ~0:07 and the back-of-head padding bleeds retention.
- **Recurring blurry handheld transition tic** — ~7/22 posts have a fully out-of-focus mid-clip frame that gives sound-off scrollers a clean exit.
- **Hashtag stuffing / typos** — recurring `#wyzbca` (meaningless), `#liehighvalley` (typo for lehighvalley), and Hail-Mary `#viral #views #blowthisup` across multiple posts. DNo7iJ6O_eY had a 26-tag stack and landed the corpus low (12/0) — cleanest evidence the stack actively suppresses reach.
- **In-video CTA: 0/22** — even when the caption has a strong CTA, no post asks for a comment, save, share, or DM on screen.
- **Caption-video format mismatch** — DNo7iJ6O_eY's caption asked "did i save him?" (implying a transformation) but the video shows only the after; the question never gets a visual payoff.

## Series / Recurring formats

- **The "recent cuts" compilation (v15 model — PROVEN, 39 likes)** — barber-selfie open + "Some of my recent cuts" frame-1 text + 3–4 strongest cuts cycled. Single best-performing format on record; only used 1/22. **Should become a weekly / bi-weekly recurring series.**
- **The silent single-client showcase (modal — 19/22)** — visual_reveal + flex_or_showcase, rotating reveals of one finished cut, 8–16s, music bed. Floor format; works reliably at 14–22 likes; lifts to 28–30 when the cut is visually striking + cover is a face-visible side profile.
- **Before / after attempts** — only 1 explicit (v17 DPSPb-diZhZ) and 1 implicit (v18 DPoAJWAjrYI with the natural-afro+hair-clip before). Both undercut by execution (weak before contrast OR no text framing). Lane is on the table but unproven.

## Goals / North Star

- **Short-term (90d):** Lift the median from ~20 to 30+ by replacing the unvaried silent template with the v15 compilation format on a weekly cadence + adding on-screen text + a face-visible-side-profile cover discipline. Drive the booking-page CTA on screen so the matured caption pitch finally converts.
- **Long-term:** Build a recognizable Lehigh Valley youth-barber brand with a documented signature format (compilation + on-screen text) and a clean booking funnel.
- **Primary metric to grow:** Likes per post (current floor 14–22, median ~20, corpus high 39 → target weekly median 30+ within 90d). Secondary: comments per post (current 0–5, median 1 → target 3+).

## Notes

- This profile is rebuilt from a complete 22-video corpus audit (2026-05-19), batches 1–5. The dominant finding to remember: **on-screen text is the binding constraint across his whole feed**; everything else (cut quality, cover, length, hashtags) is downstream.
- The "MACUNGIE BARBER • KEENAN" uploader name is a clear in-account brand asset; the v15 selfie open with on-screen text can re-use it as a logo/handle frame.
- Confirm with Dack: actual age (likely young — possibly mid-teen given the "youngbarber" hashtag), the booking app he's using, whether his TikTok / YouTube handles are also `@cutbykeenan`.

videos_jsonl.verdict_oneline: mid: Add a persistent frame-1 on-screen text hook (the cut name or a claim) so a sound-off scroller instantly knows what they are looking at — right now there is zero hook layer carrying genuinely strong work.
mid: Same single fix as the prior post — a persistent frame-1 on-screen text hook — plus stabilise the camera (kill the blurry room-swing) so every second shows the cut.
weak: Stop opening on the back of the head with the client hunched over a phone — lead with the strongest face-on or side-profile angle and add a frame-1 text hook.
mid: Cut the runtime in half — the silent showcase is fully delivered by ~00:07; everything after (repetitive back-of-head + blurry transition) only bleeds retention.
mid: Keep the personality bookend but add a frame-1 on-screen text hook and trim to ~9s — the format finally has a differentiator, now give it a readable hook and a tighter cut.
mid: Still no on-screen text and the same blurry transition tic recurs — wrap the wet/dry split with a frame-1 text label (e.g. "Wet vs dry") and cut the blur.
mid: Drop the off-subject toy-floor frame and layer in a frame-1 on-screen text hook — the face-to-camera shape works, it just needs a readable claim on top.
mid: Same standing lever (no text, no CTA, no face-to-camera) — add a frame-1 on-screen claim and the proven face-to-camera open on top of this clean execution.
mid: Surface the caption CTA + locality ON SCREEN — "Free cut in Allentown \ud83d\udc46" as frame-1 text would do twice the work of a silent showcase + buried caption.

weak: Pick the side-profile-with-face cover instead of the back-of-head, drop the three redundant back-of-head closing shots, and put the DM-for-free-cut CTA on screen — it is already in the caption, surface it.
mid: Same unchanged levers — pick the side-profile cover instead of a third back-of-head, surface the DM CTA on screen, and trim the three repetitive back-of-head closers.
strong: Even on the corpus-best post the cover is back-of-head and there is no on-screen text — both fixes would push past 30.

weak: Two fixes: (1) if the caption asks "did i save him?" the video MUST show the before — silent after-only kills the question; (2) drop the 26-hashtag spam (with typos like "liehighvalley" and "wyzbca") — a focused 5-7 tag set will out-perform a Hail-Mary stack.
mid: Cut the runtime in half — 15.7s of silent rotation is too long; the cut is delivered by 00:06 and everything after bleeds retention. Drop the phone-in-frame beat.

strong (experimental format): Put the selfie-with-text frame as the COVER (the current cover is a generic side-profile that hides the compilation hook), order the cuts by visual impact (strongest cut first), and end on an on-screen "DM 'free' to book \ud83d\udc47" — the post is one step away from compounding.
mid: v15 proved on-screen text + format change moves numbers (39 vs 20 here); reverting to the silent template a week later wastes the proof. Reapply the v15 formula immediately.

mid (experimental format): A before/after only works if the before is visibly bad — pick clients with messy / grown-out hair as the before, and use a clean hard cut (not a dark / red blur) as the transition. Cover MUST be the before with the text overlay.
mid: The exact opposite of v17 (DPSPb-diZhZ): that one had good text but weak before; this one has a great before but no text. Add the text bookends.
mid: This is v15 minus every lever that made v15 work — no selfie open, no on-screen text, no framing caption. Re-add those three things and this format lands.
strong: Already the right execution for the silent template; the gap to the corpus high (39 likes, v15) is on-screen text + a framing caption. Add those two and this is a 35+ post.
mid: Surface the booking pitch ON SCREEN with frame-1 text ("Book in bio \ud83d\udc46 Allentown") and pick a face-visible side-profile cover instead of yet another back-of-head.

weak: The strongest caption + cover decisions in the corpus paired with the corpus's hookless silent in-video standard landed at 14/0 \u2014 cleanest possible evidence the caption ceiling alone caps reach. Put the booking pitch ON SCREEN.

eb.profile_md:
# EB — Profile

_Last updated 2026-05-21 from a 4-video CIS audit corpus (batch 1 of EB's 26-reel sample, n=4 EB-originals + 1 collab repost). All sections below are grounded in audit evidence from `students/eb/videos/*/audit.md`; Brad/Dack confirms any field marked **(needs confirmation)** before it's taken as proven._

## Identity

- **Handle (Instagram, main):** **@eb.thabarber** (confirmed — Brad-supplied 2026-05-20; meta.json uploader_id `68193433826` across v3/v4/v5)
- **Handle (Instagram, twin/family):** **@twiinkutz** (EB's twin brother, also a barber — confirmed via v3 caption tag + v4/v5 comment engagement; possibly shared studio space, possibly cross-promoting bookings)
- **Handle (TikTok):** TBD — Dack confirm (hashtag `#tiktok` used in v2 collab, no direct TikTok handle visible)
- **Handle (YouTube):** TBD — Dack confirm
- **Uploader display name:** **"DC BARBER • EB"** (confirmed across v3/v4/v5 meta)
- **Real name (optional):** TBD — Dack confirm
- **Location:** **Washington, DC — Howard University area / DMV (DC-Maryland-Virginia)** (confirmed via `#dcbarber #dmvbarber #howardbarber #howarduniversity #hu26 #hu27 #hu28` hashtag clusters across all 3 confirmed posts)
- **Languages:** English (no other language audio captured in batch 1)
- **Cultural / community context:** Howard University student-barber serving the HBCU campus community + parents/visitors via house-calls and hotel-calls. Comments include personal first-name engagement ('always cheesin', 'this hotel look familiar') suggesting a tight peer/student network.

## Niche & Specialty

- **Primary niche:** **Howard University / DC college-student barber, house-call willing.** Targets the HBCU campus community as core audience (hu26/27/28 = class years).
- **Sub-specialties confirmed across the 4-audit batch:**
  - **House-call / hotel-call cuts** — unique in the CIS corpus (Yari/Jay/Cutbykeenan/George all post from fixed shops). v4 confirmed an in-room hotel cut with `#housecall` hashtag.
  - **Back-to-school / college-event themed cuts** — v3 (back-to-school Aug 18) explicitly themed for the start-of-semester rush.
  - **Glow-up / origin-story narratives** — v5 (NYE 2025) framed a long-time client's transformation as wisdom content.
- **Tools/products they're known for:**
  - Purple BAPE-camo barber cape (visible in v3 + v2 collab)
  - Portable barber tool cart (mobile rig for house-calls)
  - Ring-light stand (multi-arm rig)
  - Home/garage-style studio with comic-book wall posters (Doc Ock / Spider-Man / Thor / "Tell It Like It Is") — v2 collab + v3 v4 settings

## Archetype

- **Content archetype:** **Hybrid — personality-led narrative barber + community-flex showcase.** Across the 4-audit batch, 3 of 4 posts emphasized story/narrative (hustle, glow-up, day-in-life) over pure craft demonstration. Different lane mix from Yari (skill-heavy + community-flex) or Jay (interview format).
- **Voice / personality:** Charismatic, expressive (shock-face POV covers), narrative-driven, willing to make himself the protagonist. Big smile/cheese energy comes through in comments.
- **Tone defaults:** Entertaining / vulnerable (origin-story) / behind-the-scenes (hustle content). NOT authoritative/educational. NOT calm-demonstration. Closer to personality-creator than barber-craft expert.
- **Tier:** Coming-up — strong baseline (cover-text execution 5/5 from day one is best-in-corpus), but small audited sample. Needs more data to lock the tier.

## Audience

- **Estimated demo:** Howard University students (18-22 age range core), DMV-area young Black men + parents/visitors who book in-room cuts. Personal-name comments suggest tight network within Howard ecosystem.
- **Follower count (snapshot date — 2026-05-21):** TBD — Brad capture from IG profile manually
- **Typical engagement-per-view baseline:**
  - **EB-original posts (n=3, excluding v1 collab):** avg **203 likes / 9 comments** per post
  - **With v1 @azooore4 collab included (n=4):** avg **301 likes / 14.75 comments** per post
  - Spread: 77 (mid) → 280 (strong) on his own content; 597 outlier on collab repost
- **What they comment most:** Personal/peer engagement on EB-original posts ("always cheesin", "Not the lil look up", "this hotel look familiar") + 🔥-stack from peer barbers (twiinkutz, callmejae.b, iamchasex, youngouu_, noam.fanta). v4 drew a business-DM inquiry — first demand-gen-style comment in EB's batch ("If this is your business dm me, I wanna help with some things if I can" — sirkolbe).

## Performance baseline

- **Average likes per audited post:**
  - EB-originals: 203 / 3 posts
  - With collab v1: 301 / 4 posts
- **Top-performing format historically:**
  - Lane A (collab/entertainment skits): v1 = 597/32 — outlier reach, external content
  - Lane C (origin-story / glow-up nostalgia): v5 = 280/12 — best EB-original result
  - Lane B (day-in-the-life specialty): avg 164.5/7.5 across v3 + v4
- **Worst-performing format historically:** Lane B day-in-life when bloated (v3 at 48s with 23s of setup pulled 77/3, vs v4 at 25s tight pulled 252/12). **Length discipline matters more than format choice on this lane.**
- **Last update:** 2026-05-21 (4-audit batch 1 of 26 strategic sample)

## Strengths (what they already do well)

- **Cover-text execution 5/5 from day one.** Every single batch-1 post — collab included — puts the hook text on the cover frame. No other CIS student got this baseline locked at audit 1. This is the #1 cross-corpus coaching lever and EB has it nailed.
- **Hook strength is consistently 7-9/10 across all 4 EB-originals.** Strong visual + text combos: shock-face POV (v4), wisdom-narrative (v5), specialty-claim (v3), absurd-claim (v2 collab).
- **Unique specialty angle (house-calls/hotel-calls) differentiates from all other CIS students.** Real sales position, real demand-gen evidence already (v4 business-DM inquiry).
- **Format experimentation is healthy.** 3 distinct lanes already visible in 4 posts vs Yari's 2 lanes in 5. EB tests aggressively.
- **Tight runtime discipline** on his best posts (v2 = 19.8s, v4 = 25s, v5 = 16.4s). Knows the platform's attention budget.

## Weaknesses / Coaching Areas (where Dack is actively pushing them)

- **No actual cutting shown in any audit so far.** All 4 audits feature finished cuts, setups, and narratives — zero on-camera trimmer-in-hand / barber-process shots. Contrast: Yari/Jay/George all show the process. This is a **possible coaching opportunity** — adding 2-3 seconds of visible cutting per post would strengthen the craft signal alongside the personality content.
- **v3 long-setup bloat (`slow-setup-no-anchor`).** 23s of studio-pose intro before the first finished cut showed up in a 48s post. Pattern to watch for in future day-in-life iterations.
- **Lane B day-in-life cover-text legibility bug.** Text overlay sat on white-shirt transitions at 0:07-0:13 in v3 and became illegible. Drop-shadow / background card needed when overlays cross light backgrounds.
- **No direct booking-call-to-action in voice.** All CTAs are caption-only ("link in bio to book"). Worth testing a spoken/text booking ask in the video itself — every other CIS student shows the same pattern but the booking-funnel posts could be stronger with an in-video ask.
- **Missing on-camera demand-gen Q.** No "how'd you find me?" or "how far'd you drive?" Q&A captured in any audit yet. Yari's Lane 6 interview format proved 67% IG-attribution-capture rate; EB could add a similar 1-question beat to house-call posts.

## Series / Recurring formats

- **Lane A — Collab/entertainment skit reposts** (1 audit: v2 from @azooore4). May or may not be a recurring lane depending on how often EB shares external content — need batch 2+ data to confirm.
- **Lane B — Day-in-the-life specialty content** (2 audits: v3 back-to-school, v4 morning-college-barber). **Confirmed recurring.** Theme-based ("Back to School Cuts", "Morning as a College Barber") = repeatable monthly/per-event with new themes (Homecoming, Finals Week, Spring Break, NYE).
- **Lane C — Origin-story / glow-up nostalgia** (1 audit: v5 NYE NYE 2025). Single instance but strong execution. **Pending — needs 2nd successful run to operator-promote.**

## Goals / North Star

- **Short-term (90d):** TBD — Dack confirm. Plausible from corpus: build the Howard student bookings funnel, lean into the house-call differentiator, test if Lane C origin-story is a quarterly viable format.
- **Long-term:** TBD — Dack confirm
- **Primary metric to grow:** TBD — Dack confirm (booking volume? follower count? specialty-niche dominance in DC/DMV college barber space?)

## Notes

- **Twin context.** @twiinkutz is EB's twin brother + also a barber. v3 caption tagged him; v4/v5 comments include him. Duo-brand context, possibly shared studio space — confirm with Dack whether they cross-promote bookings.
- **Uploader-handle mismatch on collab posts.** v2 (DMdF2mhx0bp) meta says uploader = "Azure Faison" (`@azooore4`), not "DC BARBER • EB". Treat collab/repost content distinctly from EB-original when computing baselines — v1's 597 likes is external audience reach, not a fair benchmark for EB's own content.
- **v1 audit failed (DIXOODfyl_5).** yt-dlp hit IG's auth-wall on this URL only. URL stays queued; retry next batch.
- **No business-log entries yet.** When EB starts logging bookings/no-shows/revenue via `business-log` CLI, the PB Assistant Health Bar will start tracking him alongside the others.
- **Strategic-25 sample status:** 4 audited (1 + 4 = 5 attempted, 1 failed), 21 deferred in `queue.txt`, ~80% of sample remaining. Batches 2-5 will multiply EB's evidence base.

---

_Last updated: 2026-05-21 by Claude (batch-1 rewrite from 4 confirmed EB-original audits + 1 collab repost). Replaces the 2026-05-20 starter-file. Brad/Dack confirms any (needs confirmation) fields before they're taken as proven._

videos_jsonl.verdict_oneline: strong: Cut the 7-second outdoor phone-narration outro (0:11-0:18). The main visual punchline already lands at 0:05 when the client bolts; the outro repeats the joke in narration form, which dilutes the payoff and risks losing viewers who already got the laugh. A 10-12 second tight version (skit only, no outro) would likely lift retention without losing the joke.

mid: Cut the 23-second setup-before-first-cut. Frames 0-23 are EB standing in studio + slow transitions before any actual cutting shows. For a 48-second post that's HALF the runtime on non-content. Open on the 5:30pm finished cut, then jump-cut backwards to 1:30pm to set up the 'day in the life' frame, then 9:30pm closer. Also fix the text overlay drop-shadow so it stays legible across the white-shirt transitions at 0:07-0:13.

strong: The 7:00am 'Client didnt want to be recorded' beat is honest but burns the visual payoff viewers expect from a morning-grind narrative. If clients won't be on camera, replace that beat with: (1) wider shot of the full hotel-room setup (lights + cart + sculpture in frame, no client needed), (2) close-up of tool layout, (3) the building exterior with 'arriving for the call' angle. The post then keeps the hustle-arc payoff without violating client privacy. Also: the 7:30am atrium wide shot is pretty but content-empty — cut it or replace with a quick clipping-the-hair detail.

strong: The transition frame at 0:07-0:11 (close-up of young EB) is brief and the visual connection between 'EB young' and 'friend rough cut → friend now' isn't fully spelled out. Tighten with: (1) a clearer 'this is me back then' visual cue — older EB-as-teen wide shot with the friend in frame, OR (2) a quick on-screen text label like 'me, age 14' over the close-up. Otherwise viewers parse the narrative but it takes a half-second longer than it should.

hook_verbal.value: Alright bet bro alright

hook_visual.value: Client (dreadlocks, deadpan stare) sitting in a barber chair wearing a purple BAPE-camo cape, barber behind him reaching for tools, ring-light + comic-book posters on the back wall (Doc Ock / Spider-Man / Thor / 'Tell It Like It Is'). Persistent text overlay 'POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing' visible from frame 1.

value: POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing

hook_strength.value: 8/10 — the 'POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing' text claim lands at frame 1 (perfect cover-text execution), and the setup-question hook ('so how much the cut gonna be?') hits at 0:00 priming the punchline drop at 0:02. Relatable angle (every client has wondered if their barber overcharges) + the deadpan client face is scroll-stopping.

hook_payoff_seconds.value: 00:02-00:05 — the 'two bands' (= $2000) punchline lands at 0:02 and the client's immediate escape at 0:05 is the visual payoff to the 'overly taxing' setup.

value.label: Setup: client in chair, barber behind, deadpan reaction
Punchline: 'Shit gonna be like two bands' (= $2000)
Client reaction: 'Oh!'
Client bolts — barber pulls cape off, client runs out
Cut to outside at night — client on phone narrating the story
Closing voiceover: 'Boom game, hold on to game shit'

structure_loop_potential.value: no — the post ends with a closing voiceover ('hold on to game shit') and a scene change (outside on phone) that doesn't circle back to the chair-open; the joke is one-and-done.

script_cta_copy.value: [none — no spoken or visible booking ask; caption has a Twitch.tv/azo0re link but no haircut booking ask]

value.text: POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing

value: comic-book wall posters (Doc Ock / Spider-Man / Thor / 'Tell It Like It Is')

visual_setting.value: Home/garage-style barbershop setup with comic-book posters covering the back wall, ring-light rig on a stand, single barber chair — feels like a personal studio space, not a commercial shop. Outdoor closing scene shifts to a residential street at night (townhouses visible).

thumbnail_posted_describe.value: Client (dreadlocks, deadpan stare straight at camera) sitting in the barber chair wearing the purple BAPE-camo cape, with the 'POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing' text overlay legible at the bottom. Barber visible behind. Sets up the joke before viewers tap.

thumbnail_posted_strength.value: 9/10 — satisfies all 5 cover principles: (1) shows the joke setup not just a portrait, (2) carries the full hook claim as legible text, (3) sharp focal subject (deadpan client face center), (4) the 'overly taxing' tease is a perfect tap-bait setup, (5) on-brand for comedy-skit content.

value.label: Posted cover already executes principles 2 + 3 + 4 — text legible at grid size + sharp deadpan client face + 'overly taxing' tease is the perfect tap-promise; the 597-likes performance proves it earned the grid-tap

thumbnail_best_frame_reason.value: KEEP — the posted cover is the textbook example of a comedy-POV cover done right. Deadpan face + text claim + setup framing. The 597-likes performance is the proof.

content_repeatable_format.value: yes — the POV-skit-about-barbershop-experiences format is universally repeatable as a weekly comedy series (other variants: 'POV: yo barber tryna upcharge for a beard line', 'POV: yo barber wanna chop it all off', 'POV: yo barber tryna sell you product'); the structure (text claim → setup → absurd punchline → client reaction → cut-to-outside narration) is a clean template.

engagement_asks_comment.value: no — but the relatable 'every client has wondered this' angle naturally invites reaction comments (validated by the 32 comments dominated by crying-laughing emojis + 'Lmaoooo' + 'FAWWWKKK NO' echo of the punchline).

engagement_controversy_level.value: 4/10 — the 'barbers overcharge' frame is mildly polarizing (some barbers might take it personally) but the absurd punchline ($2000) signals it's a joke, not a take. Lands as humor, not as a hot take.

retention_predicted_dropoff.value: 00:11-00:18 — the cut-to-outside scene-change at frame 11 is where some viewers drop because the joke has already paid off at 0:05; the phone-narration outro is bonus material that risks losing viewers who already got the laugh. Tightening or removing the outro could lift retention.

retention_slowest_beat.value: 00:11-00:18 the outdoor phone-narration outro — adds 7 seconds of additional joke retelling after the main visual punchline already landed at 0:05.

value: 00:11-00:18 outdoor street/phone scene — leaves the barbershop setting entirely; for a barber account this drifts off-niche even though the topic stays barber-related

verdict_what_worked.value: Cover-text execution + relatable comedy frame + fast-paced skit structure. The 'POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing' text sits on the cover at frame 1, the deadpan client face is scroll-stopping, and the absurd $2000 punchline delivers within 2 seconds. 597 likes + 32 comments = strong viral-tier reach driven entirely by the joke landing.

verdict_what_to_change.value: Cut the 7-second outdoor phone-narration outro (0:11-0:18). The main visual punchline already lands at 0:05 when the client bolts; the outro repeats the joke in narration form, which dilutes the payoff and risks losing viewers who already got the laugh. A 10-12 second tight version (skit only, no outro) would likely lift retention without losing the joke.

verdict_next_video_prescription.value: Run the POV-barbershop-skit format weekly as the Reach-post slot in a 5-and-2 plan (this is the format that pulls reach beyond your follower base). Next iteration: hook_type = shock_or_surprise (text-on-cover absurd-claim setup), opening 5 words = 'POV: yo barber tryna [absurd thing]' (rotate: 'tryna upcharge for a line', 'wanna chop it all off', 'tryna sell you product', 'asks where you got that fade'), structure_arc = setup_demonstration_payoff, CTA = none (skit format doesn't need an ask), target length 10-12s (drop the outro). For Booking posts you'd need separate craft-demo content (specialty cuts, before/afters with the actual cutting visible) since this skit lane shows zero of EB's barber skill on camera.

hook_verbal.value: [silent — music bed only, no voiceover]

hook_visual.value: EB standing in his home-studio with the BAPE-camo cape draped on the barber chair, wearing a Tupac-print white tee + green pants, with persistent text overlay 'Day in the life: Back to School Cuts📚' visible at frame 1. Sets up the day-in-the-life timestamped format before the cuts roll.

value: Day in the life: Back to School Cuts📚

hook_strength.value: 7/10 — the 'Day in the life: Back to School Cuts' text on the cover is the right pattern, AND it's a strong specialty angle (Howard back-to-school = a clear target audience). Knock: at frames 7-13 the text overlay sits on top of EB's white shirt and becomes nearly illegible during the transition shots; needs a darker drop-shadow or background card.

hook_payoff_seconds.value: 00:15-00:48 — the 'back to school cuts' implied promise pays off across the three timestamped client beats (1:30pm / 5:30pm / 9:30pm) showing finished cuts throughout EB's working day.

audit_simple_md.DMdF2mhx0bp:
# Eb — Simple Audit (DMdF2mhx0bp)

_Posted 2025-07-23 · Audited 2026-05-20 · [Watch the video](https://www.instagram.com/eb.thabarber/reel/DMdF2mhx0bp/)_

**Bottom line:** This one worked well — repeat what it did.

## 1. Stop doing this
- Cut the 7-second outdoor phone-narration outro (0:11-0:18). The main visual punchline already lands at 0:05 when the client bolts; the outro repeats the joke in narration form, which dilutes the payoff and risks losing viewers who already got the laugh. A 10-12 second tight version (skit only, no outro) would likely lift retention without losing the joke.

## 2. Keep doing this
- Cover-text execution + relatable comedy frame + fast-paced skit structure. The 'POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing' text sits on the cover at frame 1, the deadpan client face is stopping the scroll, and the absurd $2000 punchline delivers within 2 seconds. 597 likes + 32 comments = strong Reach-focused-tier reach driven entirely by the joke landing.

## 3. How the video starts (first 1–3 seconds)
- How it starts: with a surprise
- First words: Alright bet bro alright
- Text on screen at the start: POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing
- How strong the opening is: 8/10 — the 'POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing' text claim lands at frame 1 (perfect cover-text execution), and the setup-question hook ('so how much the cut gonna be?') hits at 0:00 priming the punchline drop at 0:02. Relatable angle (every client has wondered if their barber overcharges) + the deadpan client face is stopping the scroll.
- When the viewer gets what was promised: 00:02-00:05 — the 'two bands' (= $2000) punchline lands at 0:02 and the client's immediate escape at 0:05 is the visual payoff to the 'overly taxing' setup.

## 4. How the video flows
- Structure: set up the story → show the cut → reveal the result
- What happens, in order: 00:00 Setup: client in chair, barber behind, deadpan reaction; 00:02 Punchline: 'Shit gonna be like two bands' (= $2000); 00:03 Client reaction: 'Oh!'; 00:05 Client bolts — barber pulls cape off, client runs out; 00:11 Cut to outside at night — client on phone narrating the story; 00:18 Closing voiceover: 'Boom game, hold on to game shit'
- Speed: fast (under 2 sec per shot)
- Does it loop nicely? no — the post ends with a closing voiceover ('hold on to game shit') and a scene change (outside on phone) that doesn't circle back to the chair-open; the joke is one-and-done.

## 5. What you said
- How many words spoken: 50
- How fast you spoke: 2.53 words per second
- Your tone: sarcastic

## 6. Text on the video
- How many text moments: 1
- What the text said: 00:00 POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing
- Caption style: small white captions

## 7. Where viewers might leave
- Most likely spot viewers leave: 00:11-00:18 — the cut-to-outside scene-change at frame 11 is where some viewers drop because the joke has already paid off at 0:05; the phone-narration outro is bonus material that risks losing viewers who already got the laugh. Tightening or removing the outro could lift retention.
- Slowest moment: 00:11-00:18 the outdoor phone-narration outro — adds 7 seconds of additional joke retelling after the main visual punchline already landed at 0:05.
- Off-topic moments: 00:11-00:18 outdoor street/phone scene — leaves the barbershop setting entirely; for a barber account this drifts off-niche even though the topic stays barber-related
- How spicy / debate-starting: 4/10 — the 'barbers overcharge' frame is mildly polarizing (some barbers might take it personally) but the absurd punchline ($2000) signals it's a joke, not a take. Lands as humor, not as a hot take.

## 8. Speed and length
- Total length: 20s
- How fast it feels: fast (under 2 sec per shot)
- How fast you spoke: 2.53 words per second

## 9. The ask (what you told viewers to do)
- Type of ask: Not recorded.
- Exact wording: [none — no spoken or visible booking ask; caption has a Twitch.tv/azo0re link but no haircut booking ask]
- Asked viewers to comment? no — but the relatable 'every client has wondered this' angle naturally invites reaction comments (validated by the 32 comments dominated by crying-laughing emojis + 'Lmaoooo' + 'FAWWWKKK NO' echo of the punchline).
- Asked viewers to save? No
- Asked viewers to share? No

## 10. How it looks (camera + setting)
- How it's shot: Client (dreadlocks, deadpan stare) sitting in a barber chair wearing a purple BAPE-camo cape, barber behind him reaching for tools, ring-light + comic-book posters on the back wall (Doc Ock / Spider-Man / Thor / 'Tell It Like It Is'). Persistent text overlay 'POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing' visible from frame 1.
- Setting: Home/garage-style barbershop setup with comic-book posters covering the back wall, ring-light rig on a stand, single barber chair — feels like a personal studio space, not a commercial shop. Outdoor closing scene shifts to a residential street at night (townhouses visible).
- People on camera: 2
- Products / brands visible: purple BAPE-camo barber cape; ring lights (multi-arm rig); barber chair; comic-book wall posters (Doc Ock / Spider-Man / Thor / 'Tell It Like It Is'); red iPhone with PopSocket case

## 11. The cover image (what shows on the grid)
- What the cover shows: Client (dreadlocks, deadpan stare straight at camera) sitting in the barber chair wearing the purple BAPE-camo cape, with the 'POV: yo barber tryna start overly taxing' text overlay legible at the bottom. Barber visible behind. Sets up the joke before viewers tap.
- How strong the cover is: 9/10 — satisfies all 5 cover principles: (1) shows the joke setup not just a portrait, (2) carries the full hook claim as legible text, (3) sharp main subject (deadpan client face center), (4) the 'overly taxing' tease is a perfect tap-bait setup, (5) on-brand for comedy-skit content.
- Does it show the finished cut? barber talking, no cut visible

- Better frame to use as the cover: KEEP Posted cover already executes principles 2 + 3 + 4 — text legible at grid size + sharp deadpan client face + 'overly taxing' tease is the perfect tap-promise; the 597-likes performance proves it earned the Instagram-grid tap — why: KEEP — the posted cover is the textbook example of a comedy-POV cover done right. - The 7:00am 'Client didnt want to be recorded' beat is honest but burns the visual payoff viewers expect from a morning-grind narrative. If clients won't be on camera, replace that beat with: (1) wider shot of the full hotel-room setup (lights + cart + sculpture in frame, no client needed), (2) close-up of tool layout, (3) the building exterior with 'arriving for the call' angle. - Hook execution + house-call hustle angle. The shock-face POV cover + 'College Barber' text + 6:45am timestamp earns the tap. **Bottom line:** This one worked well — repeat what it did.

## 1. Stop doing this
- The transition frame at 0:07-0:11 (close-up of young EB) is brief and the visual connection between 'EB young' and 'friend rough cut → friend now' isn't fully spelled out. Tighten with: (1) a clearer 'this is me back then' visual cue — older EB-as-teen wide shot with the friend in frame, OR (2) a quick on-screen text label like 'me, age 14' over the close-up. Otherwise viewers parse the narrative but it takes a half-second longer than it should.

- Origin-story / 'how I learned to cut' nostalgia narrative built around a before/after of EB's friend Yakob. george.profile_md:
# George — Profile

_Last updated 2026-05-19 from a 22-video CIS audit corpus (n=22). All metrics, archetypes, and prescription lanes below are derived from that corpus; the operator (Brad / Dack) confirms before any change is taken as proven._

## Identity

- **Handle (Instagram, main):** **@geofadeit** (confirmed from meta.json title across all 22 audits)
- **Handle (Instagram, secondary / "daily cuts" feeder):** **@cutsbyygeorge** (cross-promoted in 8+ captions; two-account strategy)
- **Handle (TikTok):** TBD — Dack confirm
- **Handle (YouTube):** TBD — Dack confirm
- **Uploader display name:** "ALLENTOWN BARBER • GEORGE"
- **Real name (optional):** George (possibly nickname "Amaar" — referenced in v5 + v14 comments by friends/family; Dack confirm)
- **Location:** **Allentown PA — Lehigh Valley** (confirmed: caption hashtags `#allentown #allentownpa #lehighvalley #lehighvalleypa #lehighvalleybarber #allentownbarber` across multiple posts + uploader name)
- **Languages:** English (some music beds in Spanish, but barber doesn't speak in any of the 22 audits)
- **Cultural context:** Tight local Muslim community network — v14 comments include Islamic phrases ("Ya Allah Zamin's son with the fresh cut", "Papa taught u well Amaar"); suggests local community ties beyond peer-barber circles. Dack confirm.

## Niche & Specialty

- **Primary niche:** Allentown / Lehigh Valley youth + young-adult barbering — moved from home/garage to a **real professional shared barbershop in July 2024**
- **Sub-specialties confirmed across the 22-video corpus:**
  - **Textured crops + low/burst/drop fades** (modal cut type, ~14 of 22 posts)
  - **Curly-hair specialist** (natural curls, fingercoil, permed curls — all visible in different posts)
  - **🚨 Cornrows + lineup** (first appearance v18 — first braiding work in the entire 85-audit CIS corpus)
  - **🚨 Perm setting / curl service** (first appearance v22 — perm rods visible mid-process)
  - **Buzz / short crops** (military-style with tight fades, v5)
- **Tools / products / equipment they're known for:**
  - **Branded pink/red "drip" T-logo clipper case + matching backpack** (v7, first brand-identity signal in the corpus)
  - **BLACK NITRILE GLOVES** (first appearance v19, professional hygiene protocol)
  - **Three documented capes:** pink/blue camo (v1–v15, same pattern as peer Cutbykeenan — Lehigh Valley shared aesthetic), Louis Vuitton monogram (v9, aspirational/fashion-conscious), and **black "CAPE" branded with neon yellow/green neck strip** (v17 onward, current default at the new shop)
  - **Wall mirror + ring light** for content production (visible from v4 onward — dedicated content equipment, a step above peer Cutbykeenan)

## Archetype

- **Content archetype:** **Format-experimenting community_flex / authority_demonstration barber** — by far the highest departure rate in the CIS (5/22 = 23%; Cutbykeenan 9%, Jay 4%). Modal is silent showcase but actively explores new formats monthly.
- **Voice / personality:** Visual-only (barber doesn't speak in any of 22 posts), but **self-features on camera more than any other student** (1 BTS self-cut v2, multiple selfie covers/opens v7+v11) — visible personality without verbal voiceover.
- **Tone defaults:** Calm demonstration (modal); hype for departures (compilation, meme cold open).
- **Tier:** **Solo home-barber → shared pro-shop transition in July 2024** — actively maturing business in the audited period. Self-positions as `#beginnerbarber` (v14) and `#youngbarber` (implied) — humble framing, not yet a senior name.

## Audience

- **Estimated demo:** Lehigh Valley youth + young-male client base (visible across audits: kids, teens, young adults). Two distinct audience strata: **(a) peer barbers** (recurring commenters `luisthegbarber`, `blakenassry`, `baby.rxcksss`, `kbabyblendz`, `racklessbarber`, `omxr.aaa`) — **the densest peer-barber engagement in the entire CIS corpus**; **(b) local Muslim / immigrant community** (v14 Islamic-phrase comments; "Zamin's son" community-name references). His engagement skews professional-peer + tight-knit-local, not generic-IG-audience.
- **Follower count (snapshot date — YYYY-MM-DD):** TBD — not exposed in post metadata. Dack confirm.
- **Typical engagement-per-view baseline:** Views are hidden in IG metadata (-1 for all 22 posts). Likes range **18–74**, median **~46**, with two clusters: pre-shop (~50–67 median for typical+text and departures) and post-shop (~22 median for typical-no-text). Comments range **0–11** (median 1).
- **What they comment most:** Peer-barber craft critique ("you could've brought his back taper up higher cuz of how long his hair grows in his neck" — v5), peer admiration ("Time to retire fr 😭🙏" — v6, "U him bou", "U too tuff", "king shit george"), real CLIENT testimonials ("You got me right bro", "Bro saved me 🙏", "Bro blessed him up 🙏"), and transformation-question reactions ("From homeless to ho3less 🔥", "He was ugly as fuck before but now you got him right" — v12). **The peer-barber-critique pattern is rare across the CIS** and is George's untapped engagement lever.

## Performance baseline

- **Average likes per post (22-video corpus):** mean ~38, median **~46**, range 18–74
- **Average comments per post:** mean ~2.3, median 1, range 0–11 (8/22 posts landed 0 comments)
- **Top-performing format historically:** **🚨 Meme/viral-reference cold open** — n=1 (v11 C6P0gVfubMX, 74 likes / 11 comments — corpus high on BOTH metrics across ALL FOUR students). Screen-recorded GTA car-crash meme → cut reveal → self-deprecating caption ("did i mess him up⁉️") tying the two together.
- **Second-best format:** **Before/after transformation with on-screen text** — n=3 proven (v6 70, v12 64, v13 56, averaging 63); the text adds ~+6–10 over the same format without text.
- **Third format:** **"Come with me" mobile vlog with branded gear** — n=1 (v7 64 likes), with deliberate all-text serif cover.
- **Fourth format:** **Multi-cut compilation with hand-on-hair + text** — n=1 with text (v3, 49); n=1 without (v16, 28) — confirms the hook stack matters.
- **Worst-performing format historically:** **Silent typical showcase without on-screen text + back-of-head cover + dim lighting** (v15 25/1, v8 39/0, v9 49/2, v16 28/0, v17 18/0, v18 23/0). The 6-post shop-move window (v17–v22) is the worst engagement period despite the biggest business upgrades — confirms text+cover > setting+craft.
- **Last update:** 2026-05-19 (n=22 corpus encoding by CIS, operator-approved).

## Strengths

- **🚨 Highest format-experimentation rate in the CIS** (5 departures / 22 audits = 23% — vs Cutbykeenan 9%, Jay 4%). Actively explores new lanes monthly: before/after, vlog, compilation, meme cold open, self-cut BTS. Each departure he tries is structurally different, not just repeating one — real creative range.
- **🚨 Multi-specialty service portfolio** — three documented skill lanes (standard cuts + cornrows + perms) with cornrows being the first braiding work in the entire 85-audit CIS corpus and perms being the second specialty added in the shop-move window.
- **Two-account content strategy** — main @geofadeit (hero posts) + secondary @cutsbyygeorge (daily cuts feeder), cross-promoted in 8+ captions. Real content-strategy maturity, unique in the CIS.
- **Real brand identity** — pink "drip" T-logo gear (clipper case + matching backpack), three documented capes (camo, LV, current branded black-with-neon).
- **Dedicated content equipment** — ring light, wall mirror, professional shop with mounted ring lights, camera setup. A step above peers (Cutbykeenan has none).
- **Business stage maturation in audited period** — caption arc went from no offer → "free cuts tap in" → "DM for free cuts" → "$10 cuts" → "follow @cutsbyygeorge daily" → "Collab👀" → "booking app/page in my bio" → "open next week" → "spots open next week" → "1 spot open wednesday". Clear maturation from free-cuts-only marketing to appointment-based scheduling.
- **Moved from garage to real shared pro shop in July 2024** — major business event in the audited period.
- **Densest peer-barber audience in the CIS** — multiple recurring barber commenters; peer-network drives the highest comment counts (v5 7, v11 11). The peer-craft-debate is his single highest-leverage engagement mechanic.

## Weaknesses / Coaching Areas

- **🚨 On-screen text is the binding constraint across the corpus** — 14/22 typical posts have NO on-screen text; these average ~33 likes vs ~58 for posts with text. Single most predictable engagement lever, repeatedly un-pulled in typical-format posts.
- **🚨 Wasted brand-event announces** — the shop move (v17), the gloves debut (v19), the cornrows debut (v18), the perms debut (v22) — each a one-time announceable moment, none labeled on screen. The shop-move window (v17–v22) lost half his pre-shop median because none of those events got a frame-1 text hook.
- **Recurring back-of-head cover habit** — 9+ posts opt for back-of-head over the face-visible side-profile that's available in the same clip. Lowest-effort fix.
- **Runtime creep on some posts** — 3 posts at 15–25s (v2, v7, v14, v16) all engagement-cap below 50; the silent-template payoff is delivered by ~7s and everything after bleeds retention.
- **Hashtag-typo regression** — `#lehighvallypa`, `#lehighvallybarbers` (missing 'e') in v16. Otherwise the locality+niche hashtag discipline is generally clean (much cleaner than peer Cutbykeenan's `#wyzbca` typo + `#viral #views` spam).
- **In-video CTA: 0/22** — every CTA evolved in captions only; never surfaced on screen even when the caption strategy matured (urgency, collab, booking-app, appointment slots, multi-channel).
- **Brand-safety risk surfaced once** — v13 drew a peer body-shaming comment about the client ("U cut his hair but he still needa cut some weight 👏") — the first negative client-directed comment in the entire 85-audit CIS corpus. Tight crops on the cut (avoiding body-emphasis framing) would mitigate.

## Series / Recurring formats

- **🚨 "Recent cuts" multi-client compilation with hand-on-hair selfie open** (v3 model — PROVEN, 49 likes): hand on client's hair + frame-1 "haircuts I did today 💈" text + 4-5 strongest cuts cycled. Should run weekly/bi-weekly as a recurring volume post.
- **🚨 Before/after transformation with on-screen "did i save him?" text** (v6 model — PROVEN n=3, average 63): BEFORE on cover with text label → mid-cut → AFTER reveal → caption-question tie-in. Should run monthly or per dramatic-before client. **Critically: this is the EXACT format Cutbykeenan attempted and failed at** (caption asked transformation but video only showed after); George executes it right (shows both halves) and lands corpus highs. A documented A/B in the CIS.
- **🚨 Meme/viral cold-open + cut reveal** (v11 model — PROVEN single best, 74/11): screen-record a viral reel for 2s → pivot to barber setting → cut reveal → self-deprecating caption ties them ("did I mess him up?"). Borrowed-attention mechanic; novel in the niche.
- **🚨 "Come with me" mobile-service / BTS vlog** (v7 model — PROVEN 64): all-text serif cover + barber-selfie open + branded supplies + travel/setup + finished cut. Series candidates: "come with me to a {school/lunch/event/house} cut", "come with me on a packed day".
- **Modal silent showcase** (10+ posts): visual_reveal + flex_or_showcase + face-visible side profile + frame-1 text + locality+niche hashtags + appointment CTA. The floor format that reliably clears ~50 when the text lever is on, drops to ~25 when off.

## Goals / North Star

- **Short-term (90d):** Lift the median from ~46 to ~60+ by enforcing on-screen text on every typical post + running v6/v11/v7 departures as monthly recurring series + announcing the new shop / specialties (cornrows, perms) with proper hooks. Get the shop-move window's lost engagement back.
- **Long-term:** Build a recognizable Lehigh Valley youth+specialty barber brand with documented signature formats (compilation + before/after + vlog + meme cold open) and a clean booking funnel. The peer-barber audience is unique enough to support a "barber's barber" tier.
- **Primary metric to grow:** Likes per post (current median ~46, top 74 — target weekly median 60+ within 90d). Secondary: comments per post (current 0–11, median 1 → target 5+ on departures, leveraging the peer-barber-critique mechanic).

## Notes

- Profile rebuilt from a complete 22-video corpus audit (2026-05-19), batches 1–5. The dominant pattern: **George's craft and business sophistication is the highest of the three peer-tier students (Yari/Jay/Cutbykeenan), but his typical-format engagement still depends on the same on-screen-text + face-visible-cover levers Cutbykeenan also misses**.
- **Cross-corpus connection:** `@geofadeit` (George) is the same handle that recurringly fire-emoji-comments on multiple Cutbykeenan posts (DM3bhDiJJ2f, DK429T7uvdL, DPoAJWAjrYI, DNYJPVtO7Wi). George and Cutbykeenan are **connected Lehigh Valley barbers** (George = Allentown, Keenan = Macungie), use the same pink/blue camo cape pattern. The Lehigh Valley barber-peer network is real and George is a node in it.
- **Confirm with Dack:** real name (is it Amaar or George?), follower counts for both accounts, age (likely young — `#beginnerbarber` + `#youngbarber` framing), whether his TikTok / YouTube handles also follow the @geofadeit / @cutsbyygeorge pattern, and whether the new shop is solo or shared (another barber visible in v20 mirror reflection suggests shared).

## Niche & Specialty

- **Primary niche:** Barber / men's grooming — niche-tight in ~22 of 23 audits
- **Sub-specialties:** Fades (drop / burst / low / mid / taper), lineups & hairline "enhancements", **loc/dread retwist & styling**, cornrows/braids, big chops, curly & textured tops/fringes, blunt-fringe/bowl. Genuinely all-hair-types — unusually versatile for his age; the **loc/curly** work is a differentiated strength and a consistent upper-mid performer.
- **Tools/products:** Stylecraft (Saber trimmer), BabylissPro, Wahl Magic Clip, Gamma, gold T-blade trimmer, 360 waves work (recurring hashtags/frames)
- **Booking:** "TheCut" app, link in bio (actively monetizing); relocated mid-2025 ("first cut in new location") to a ring-lit home studio.

## Archetype

- **Content archetype:** Skill-heavy young barber whose **breakout lane is captioned interview / transformation storytelling**. (Revised from the n=9 "silent showcase" read — the talking/interview format is now empirically his best content, see Performance.)
- **Voice / personality:** Two registers — (a) silent showcase montages set to rap/R&B; (b) on-camera **barber-chair interview banter** (entertaining, "bro"-heavy, surfaces the client's grievance). Personality historically buried in the caption; lands hardest when it's on-screen.
- **Tone defaults:** Showcase = `calm_demonstration`; interview = `entertaining`.
- **Tier:** Developing-with-a-proven-ceiling. Cutting skill is strong and *constant* across all 23; the 10× performance spread is entirely a packaging/hook variable, not craft.

## Audience

- **Estimated demo:** Heavily other barbers / the barber community (commenter handles are barbers; recurring: noex3, jr_randel, jori.bxndz, ajomertaa, mikeygtfm_; cousin yaricutz; coach cutbydack). Peer-validation + a real local Allentown booking audience (interview videos pull "where you from" locals).
- **Follower count:** TBD — Dack confirm
- **Typical engagement-per-view baseline:** Unmeasurable — views hidden on every post (–1). Likes/comments only.
- **What they comment most:** Emoji (🔥/👏) on showcases; on the interview format, real replies/tags and barber-comparison talk (drives 13–39 comments vs the 2–9 emoji baseline).

- **Likes:** mean ≈ 94, median 64, range **34–333**
- **Comments:** mean ≈ 9.5, range **2–39**
- **Views:** hidden on all posts (–1)

- **Top performers (likes):** `DUnsP8wjgsO` 333 · `DWBtpE_DhhU` 184 · `DVB3YTkjuEw` 179 · `DWFzMk5jiII` 173 · `DJ7RgqwOuWA` 142 · `DTN8ozIDrFd` 123
- **Top performers (comments):** `DWBtpE_DhhU` 39 · `DUnsP8wjgsO` 30 · `DTN8ozIDrFd` 16 · `DWFzMk5jiII` 13 · `DLWPL3OOvrF`/`DVB3YTkjuEw` 12
- **Floor:** `DQdJCdmjqPU` 34 · `DRQLrbuDj9c` 37 · `DKco8bnOY8r` 41 · `DVeMPrZjmIh` 43 — all no-text silent showcases (or a label with a fatal pacing flaw)

- **Last update:** 2026-05-19 (full n=23 CIS audit corpus)

## Strengths

- Genuinely versatile, consistent cutting skill at 15 across every hair type (locs, curly/afro, fades, braids, big chops, blunt fringe).
- **A proven, repeatable flagship format he can already execute** (captioned interview/problem-solve — 3/3 top-tier).
- Strong finished-result covers on the showcase lane; his best covers carry on-screen credential text.
- Working narrative instincts — when the hook is a question/problem/credential, engagement multiplies 2–5×.
- Demonstrably **coachable** (applied the frame-1 anchor note when given it) — but inconsistently (see Weaknesses #1).
- Access to produced studio (dual ring lights, LED mirror) + a collaborator (cousin Yari) + direct coach attention (Dack comments).

## Weaknesses / Coaching Areas

1. **Hook-discipline is intermittent, not habitual (the #1 ceiling).** The on-screen anchor/hook oscillated on→off through *every* breakthrough (DQyALLnjh-I → DRQLrbuDj9c off; DUnsP8wjgsO 333 → DV6qPC4Gogl off). Left to default he reverts to the silent no-text showcase. Must become a fixed, non-optional template.
2. **Over-long static "before" / talk-before-cut.** Recurring across ~10 months: DJOe9CQOTYu (7s before) → DVeMPrZjmIh (8s motionless before, floored at 43/2); interview intros run 24–26s before the cut on 38–47s clips.
3. **Slow / ambiguous / blurry openings & covers** on the showcase lane (ring-light, tilted-at-wall, blurry washout covers as low as 1/10).
4. **Caption-not-screen** for hooks/CTAs when not doing the interview format.
5. **No on-screen CTA** outside the interview lane — leaves comment volume on the table.
6. **Runtime bloat** — 37–72s no-/low-payoff montages; even strong interviews run long.
7. Inconsistent loop construction (only the silent showcase loops cleanly; it's also his weakest lane).

## Series / Recurring formats

_(ranked by proven performance — this ranking is the coaching playbook)_

1. **🥇 FLAGSHIP — Captioned barber-chair interview → problem-solve** (`story` hook / `problem_agitate_solve`). Client interviewed in-chair, surfaces a grievance ("my last barber messed up my line / corners"), Jay fixes it; sticker auto-captions throughout. **3/3 top-tier: DUnsP8wjgsO 333/30, DWBtpE_DhhU 184/39, DWFzMk5jiII 173/13.** His single best, repeatable format. Shared with cousin Yari. **Do weekly.**
2. **🥈 Credential multi-cut best-of** (`authority` / `flex_or_showcase`). Frame-1 "Some recent cuts as a 15 yr old barber ⭐" over a curated 6-8 cut reel. DVB3YTkjuEw 179/12 (strong); DJ7RgqwOuWA 142/6.
3. **🥉 "Came in after N months of wolfing" transformation** (`before_after` / `setup_demonstration_payoff`, on-screen story hook). DTN8ozIDrFd 123/16 (strong).
4. **Labeled before/after enhancement** (`setup_demonstration_payoff`). Works only with a 2-3s before; the over-long static before guts it (DVeMPrZjmIh 43/2).
5. **Silent process→reveal showcase** (`flex_or_showcase`) — his historical default and **entire weak floor**. Retire, or only post with a proven on-screen hook layered on.

## Goals / North Star

- **Short-term (90d):** Inferred — reach + barber-community cosigns + local bookings ("TheCut" app, #15yroldbarber, big-barber tags). Dack confirm.
- **Long-term:** TBD — Dack confirm
- **Primary metric to grow:** Reach + comments. The proven lever is now specific and ranked: **run the captioned interview/problem-solve format as the weekly flagship**; rotate the credential best-of and wolfing transformation; retire the silent no-text showcase.

**Bottom line:** This one was okay — fixable, not a winner yet.

# Jay — Simple Audit (DWBtpE_DhhU)

_Posted 2026-03-18 · Audited 2026-05-19 · [Watch the video](https://www.instagram.com/reel/DWBtpE_DhhU/)_

**Bottom line:** This one worked well — repeat what it did.

## Dack-approved scripts this matches
- **Jay — "Came in after N months of wolfing → I said bet" transformation** — On-screen story hook over the rough before, tight process, clean dropped-loc/finished reveal. **Rule: on-screen story hook + a real visible delta + ≤3s before. Proven: DTN8ozIDrFd 123/16 (strong).** _(CIS-derived examples=23 Jay audit; Dack-approved 2026-05-19.)_
- _(This is one of Dack's approved scripts. The notes below give it extra weight, but if the numbers disagree, the numbers win.)_

## 1. Stop doing this
- Strong — optimize, don't rework: tighten the interview intro and the cut/detailing middle to bring 38s down to ~25-30s, and carry the 'my last barber messed me up' hook line onto the cover (currently text-less) for the grid tap.

## 2. Keep doing this
- He repeated the proven captioned barber-chair interview/problem-solve format ('my last barber messed me up, look at my corners → I got you, you ready?') — and it delivered again: 184 likes (2nd-highest ever) and 39 comments (his all-time highest), proving the format is a repeatable winner, not a one-off.

## 3. How the video starts (first 1–3 seconds)
- How it starts: with a story
- First words: What's up where you coming
- Text on screen at the start: WHAT'S UP BRO WHERE YOU; FROM ALLENTOWN BRO
- How strong the opening is: 8/10 — a spoken barber-chair interview with bold auto-captions that quickly surfaces a relatable problem ('my last barber messed me up, look at my corners'); talking + on-screen text from frame 1 is his proven top hook, and the engagement confirms it.
- When the viewer gets what was promised: 00:33 — the clean curly-top + square hairline + drop-fade finished reveal answers the 'he messed up my corners' problem.

## 4. How the video flows
- Structure: show the problem → make it feel painful → fix it
- What happens, in order: 00:00 interview: where from / how far; 00:06 client problem: 'last barber messed me up, look at my corners'; 00:11 'trying to get you to bless me' / 'you should just cook bro'; 00:18 'I got you bro, you ready?' — challenge accepted; 00:20 cut begins, music in, curls clipped; 00:28 fade + hairline carving (process); 00:33 finished reveal: curly top + square hairline + drop fade; 00:38 closing finished profile
- Speed: uneven pacing
- Does it loop nicely? No — it opens on the interview 2-shot and ends on the finished solo profile; a problem→solve arc doesn't loop.

## 5. What you said
- How many words spoken: 90
- How fast you spoke: 2.34 words per second
- Your tone: entertaining

## 6. Text on the video
- How many text moments: 11
- What the text said: 00:00 WHAT'S UP BRO WHERE YOU; 00:01 FROM ALLENTOWN BRO; 00:03 LIKE 10 MINUTES; 00:06 I DON'T KNOW BRO; 00:07 HE LOWKEY MESSED; 00:08 MY CORNERS RIGHT NOW; 00:11 BLESS ME; 00:12 NOT GONNA LIE YOU; 00:14 TO DO WITH THAT BRO; 00:18 GET ME RIGHT BRO; 00:19 YEAH BRO
- Caption style: yellow/black CapCut-style captions

## 7. Where viewers might leave
- Most likely spot viewers leave: 00:20-00:30 — after the strong interog hook, the cut/detailing middle runs long on a 38s clip; the bleed is in the process stretch before the reveal, not the hook.
- Slowest moment: 00:22-00:30 — repetitive clipping/hairline detailing with little new information; lowest info-per-second between the interview and the reveal.
- Off-topic moments: None.
- How spicy / debate-starting: 4/10 — the 'last barber messed me up' framing invites barber-vs-barber debate and relatability; his most comment-engineered angle, replicated.

## 8. Speed and length
- Total length: 38s
- How fast it feels: uneven pacing
- How fast you spoke: 2.34 words per second

## 9. The ask (what you told viewers to do)
- Type of ask: Not recorded.
- Exact wording: No spoken or explicit ask.
- Asked viewers to comment? No explicit ask, but the relatable 'my last barber messed me up' problem strongly invites switch-barber/reaction comments — it drove 39, his all-time highest comment count.
- Asked viewers to save? No
- Asked viewers to share? No

## 10. How it looks (camera + setting)
- How it's shot: A 2-shot: Jay in a grey hoodie holding a mic/clipper standing beside a seated client with an overgrown curly top in a gold cape, home studio with a basketball game on the TV, bold sticker caption 'WHAT'S UP BRO WHERE YOU' across the bottom.
- Setting: A produced home studio with ring lights, an LED mirror and a wall TV showing a basketball game; daytime.
- People on camera: 2
- Products / brands visible: clipper; ring lights; vintage-car print cape; LED mirror

## 11. The cover image (what shows on the grid)
- What the cover shows: A clean side/top-down shot of the finished curly top with a sharp square hairline and clean drop fade in the ring-lit studio, no text overlay.
- How strong the cover is: 7/10 — a strong well-lit after-result with a clear subject, but the winning interview/problem hook isn't represented on the cover (no text), so the tap rests on craft alone.
- Does it show the finished cut? the finished cut
- Is the text readable on the grid? no text on cover
- Is the subject clear? one clear subject
- Better frame to use as the cover: 00:08 the interview 2-shot with the 'MY CORNERS RIGHT NOW' problem caption — sets up the story/curiosity that drove the record engagement, which the text-less finished cover doesn't — why: Swapping to ~00:08 carries the problem-line hook as caption text (principle 2) and a 'can he fix it?' curiosity gap (principle 4); the posted finished cover resolves the transformation before the tap.

## 12. The caption
- What the caption said: "LINK IN BIO TO BOOK📲

#reels #barber #xycba #clippers #hair"
- Hashtags used: #barber, #clippers, #hair, #reels, #xycba
- Link / ask in the caption: link in bio
- Location tag: None.

## 13. How it did (likes / comments / views)
- When this was audited: 184 likes, 39 comments, hidden views.
- No follow-up check yet.
- How this one stacks up for Jay: This one worked well — repeat what it did.
- How it compares to your usual: A twist on one of your usual formats.
- What this post is for: booking post (made to get bookings)

# Yari — Profile

- **Real name (optional):** TBD — Dack confirm
- **Business name (confirmed 2026-05-20):** **Lincoln Barrow** — Yari's named shop (first business-brand-name surfaced in the entire 85-audit CIS corpus). Mentioned by voiceover in v1 ("you know who to book with, Lincoln Barrow") and visible across all 10 batch-1+batch-2 audits as the consistent venue. **Venue is now two-zone:** PRIMARY cutting area (pink/magenta geometric-lattice backdrop, pink LED accent) + SECONDARY daylit studio (white walls, floating shelves, photo softbox lighting) — secondary studio confirmed across v8 + v9 + v10 as Yari's content-production space for interviews and brand portraits.
- **Signature offering (NEW, captured 2026-05-20 from v10):** **"The Yari special"** — a NAMED SIGNATURE CUT emerging in client vernacular. The Miami-flight client in v10 spontaneously said: *"I came over here to get the Yari special, bro."* First branded-vernacular phrase in the entire 85-audit CIS corpus. Should be claimed: name it on the booking page, mention it in voiceover, prompt clients to repeat it on camera.
- **Location:** Allentown, PA (📍ALLENTOWN PA pin in every 2026 caption — confirmed across all 10 batch audits)
- **Languages:** English (primary voiceover) + Spanish (audio-bed reggaeton in v5 + PR-flag visual in v4 → Latin/PR-diaspora cultural layer is real, not incidental)
- **Personal brand styling (captured 2026-05-20 from v6, v8, v10):** Yari herself appears on-camera in interview-format posts wearing **gold rope chain + gold medallion pendant + gold cuban link bracelet** (every batch-2 audit she's in-frame, same jewelry stack) + visible Latin-style forearm tattoos. Wardrobe rotates (navy long-sleeve v8, white printed cowboy/Western-motif long-sleeve v10); the gold-jewelry stack is the visual brand-constant.

## Niche & Specialty

- **Primary niche:** Custom cuts — high-skill men's styling with a hard specialty-positioning play (2026)
- **Sub-specialties:**
  - **"Specialist" positioning claims** (NEW 2026 — templated weekly Mad Lib): "number one **cowlick** specialist in Allentown" (v1), "number one **hairline** specialist in Allentown" (v3) — same verbatim script with the specialty noun swapped. Format-bank for future videos: curl, fade, beard, lineup, kid-cut, mullet, taper, braids, perm.
  - Hairline / lineup work — squared-off, V-shaped widow's peak (proven across 2023 + 2026 sample)
  - Tied-back / braided styles — man-bun with locs, cornrow/feed-in braids, twisted dreadlocks (visible in v5 sample)
  - Stitch-braid / twist styles
- **Tools/products they're known for:**
  - Wahl trimmer with blue LED (visible v3)
  - BaBylissPRO
  - L3VEL3 products
  - Gold foil shaver
  - Black nitrile gloves (visible v2, v3 — hygiene signal)
  - **KB46279… white cape** (NEW — Yari's signature cape, branded with reg code, visible in v2/v3/v5 — looks like a barber license # or shop registration code. Consistent brand prop.)

## Archetype

- **Content archetype:** Multi-lane skill-heavy + brand-storytelling barber, operating in **SEVEN confirmed lanes** (expanded 2026-05-20 across batches 1+2+3 from prior 2-lane scaffold):
  1. **Specialty-claim voiceover demo** (avg 150 likes / 15.4 comments across 5 iterations) — talks to camera with templated "number one X specialist in Allentown" script + problem_agitate_solve narrative + visible process showcase + booking CTA. Refined as of v9 with **Hazleton-distance social-proof hook** + **client-spoken testimonial outro**. **v11 added a THIRD-PARTY-VOICED variant**: "they don't call me the hairline specialist for no reason" (vs first-party "I am X"). Proven by v1 (cowlick 153/6), v3 (hairline 171/21), v7 (taper 55/10 capped by no-face cover), **v9 (hairline+testimonial 237/22 — corpus likes high)**, **v11 (big-chop third-party-voiced 134/18 — first Dack comment on this lane)**. **🚨 PROVABLY CONVERTING BOOKINGS** — v10's Miami-flight client cited this lane as why he booked. Now Operator-proven in `head/proven_scripts.md` as FLAGSHIP.
  2. **Silent showcase with on-screen value-prop text** — no voiceover, music bed only, single value-prop quote on cover ("BRING BACK MY CONFIDENCE") + multi-angle lineup beauty showcase. Proven by v2 DXvDA_kOL2j (71/13 with peer-network density — Jay + George both commented).
  3. **Origin-story / brand-narrative montage** (DACK-AMPLIFIED) — on-screen text narrative ("Yea I've been cutting for 5 years ... But i'll never forget ...") + present-day Lincoln Barrow cover + multi-venue throwback footage (garage cut, outdoor street cut with PR flag, beginner bedroom-vanity setup). Proven by v4 DXp-uv5Do6_ (122/10) — **drew the first ever DACK COMMENT on a student post in the entire CIS corpus** ("dito 😿" from @cutbydack).
  4. **"Couldn't Save" humor + roll-call showcase** (DACK-AMPLIFIED — CONFIRMED RECURRING SERIES, 3 iterations spanning 9 days). Now Operator-proven in `head/proven_scripts.md`. Relatable-humor text hook + ironic punchline + saved-clients gallery + booking-funnel caption. Proven: **v14 DXQTJljjlF_** (chronologically FIRST, 2026-04-18, 82/14 — first-iteration weakness pattern), **v6 DXk2RR9jmtd** (2026-04-26, 99/**25 corpus comments high** + Dack + first long-form fan comment from @ryansmith6543), **v5 DXnc-wVjouX** (2026-04-27, 88/13 + Dack). Avg **89.7 likes / 17.3 comments** across 3 iterations. Dack-amplification 2/3 (v5+v6 yes, v14 no — first iteration missed).
  5. **Trending-audio ring-light "satisfying clean-up" showcase** (HISTORICAL VIRAL — not active in 2026 sample) — no speech, rides a current trending sound, ring-light beauty framing, satisfying clean-up reveal. Proven viral by C0P0u40Ps9y (**56,926 likes**, 2023-11-29) and Czfp5m5OEy3 (**24,297 likes**, 2023-11-11). **Dormant across both batch 1 + batch 2 (10 audits)** — open question for Dack: re-introduce this lane weekly?
  6. **Jay-import mock barber-chair interview** (CONFIRMED RECURRING SERIES, **NOW YARI'S MODAL FORMAT** — 4 iterations). Now Operator-proven in `head/proven_scripts.md`. Two-mic press-interview rig + captioned dialog + client-articulated problem + Yari's prescriptive plan + cut transformation. Jay endorsed with 7-fire on v8 (his highest-emoji Yari comment ever). **Demand-gen "how you find out about me?" Q captured Instagram-attribution on 2 of 3 iterations** (v10 Miami flight + v12 "Instagram, bro"; v13 missed it). Proven: v8 (ski-mask, 76/13 + Jay 7-fire), v10 (Miami flight + "Yari special" vernacular, 185/20), v12 (downtown + Instagram-attribution, 92/15), v13 (warm rapport + **`@trifactorscaling` first corpus comment**, 108/21). Avg **115 likes / 17.25 comments** across 4 iterations. Format consolidating — two "typical" verdicts in a row (v12+v13).
  7. **🆕 Self-cut POV / personal vulnerability** (NEW LANE discovered v15 — n=1; **NEW CORPUS COMMENTS HIGH 41**, beats v6's 25 by 64%). Yari as the SUBJECT of the cut (not operator) + POV-text hook ("POV YOU KNOW HOW TO CUT EVERYONE'S HAIR BUT YOURS 😢") + Spanish ballad audio bed + cover-text-on-cover execution. **Pulls a completely different audience subset**: female-coded fan engagement dominates ("Cute as hell", heart emojis, "Dm me") vs the peer-barber 🔥-stack pattern of Lanes 1-6. Proven: **v15 DXN0VRzDuCg (185/41 — corpus comments high)**. Pending entry candidate after a 2nd successful run. Variants to try: "POV being a Latina barber in Allentown", "POV your favorite barber is a girl", "POV when your client asks for the impossible cut".

- **Voice / personality:** Confident, competitive, lightly combative — positions self as the specialty authority who fixes what other barbers ruin ("your barber don't do the correct line of prep" v3). Now ALSO operates a relatable-humor lane (v5 "the clients I couldn't save") and a vulnerable-origin-story lane (v4 "I'll never forget") — the brand voice has range, not just one tone.
- **Tone defaults:** `confident_authority` on specialty-voiceover; `calm_demonstration` on silent showcases; `reflective_nostalgic` on origin-story; `playful_humor` on roll-call. English confirmed for spoken content; Spanish confirmed for audio beds.
- **Tier:** **Strong** — 4 of 5 newest audits rated strong, 1 mid, no weak posts in the 2026 sample. Lincoln Barrow shop + booking funnel + multi-lane brand work = a real local-business creator, not just a content account.

## Audience

- **Estimated demo:** Allentown-area + Lehigh Valley local clients (caption pins, peer comments from `andresdabarber` `flippin448s_` `leongonzallez` `geofadeit` `jayvblends` `x.percel` `archivekah` `j.santana._._` etc — almost all peer barbers from the same region) + PR/Latin diaspora (v4 PR-flag visual, v5 Spanish reggaeton bed, family ties to Jay per CLAUDE.md "cousins" note).

## Performance baseline

_(refreshed 2026-05-20 after batches 1+2+3 of strategic-sample audit — 18 prior 2023-era audits + 15 newest 2026 = 33 total)_

**🚨 #1 CROSS-LANE LEVER PROVEN (v15, 2026-05-20):** put the joke/hook text **ON the cover frame**, not just inside the clip. All 14 prior batch audits flagged this as the highest-leverage repeat coaching item; every cover landed without it. v15 finally executed it (POV text overlaid on the cover) → landed 41 comments (corpus high, +64% over prior peak). **Apply this pattern to every future Yari post regardless of lane.**

## Weaknesses / Coaching Areas

- **🚨 NO-FACE COVER IS A CAUSAL LIKES-KILLER (NEW EVIDENCE 2026-05-20)** — v7 DXiURrHDkyO shipped with an extreme-close-up cover showing only the lineup precision (no face, no clear subject). Result: **55 likes** vs the 71-237 range every other 2026 post hit. **53% drop vs the running 117-like mean.** n=1 anecdotal but magnitude is large enough to treat as CAUSAL, not correlational. **Rule: every Yari cover must be face-visible.**
- **Cover-frame text overlay still missing in 9 of 10 batch audits** — universal weakness, despite text overlays appearing IN-clip in 6 of 10 (v2, v4, v5, v6, v8, v10). The cover/thumbnail is where grid-tap is won, and Yari's covers rely on cut-quality alone. Single highest-leverage repeat coaching item across the entire 2026 sample. Specific lane fixes:
  - Specialty-voiceover (v1, v3, v7, v9) → "HAIRLINE FIX" / "COWLICK FIX" / "WORTH AN HOUR 30 DRIVE"
  - Silent-text-flex (v2) → already has in-clip text; move/duplicate to cover
  - Origin-story (v4) → "5 YEARS IN"
  - Couldn't-save (v5, v6) → the "Clients I couldn't save..." hook IS the hook — put it on cover
  - Interview (v8, v10) → bombshell-headline (v8: "WHAT'S UNDER THE SKI MASK?"; v10: "I FLEW FROM MIAMI")
- **Trending-audio viral lane dormant across 10 batch audits** — Yari's only 2 viral hits (24K, 57K) are this format and 0 of the 10 newest sample posts use it. Two interpretations: (a) Yari shifted focus deliberately to local-booking content (which makes business sense, given v10's provable booking-conversion evidence), or (b) drift away from the viral lane is leaving reach on the table. **Worth a Dack conversation.**
- **Talk-to-cut gap on interview lane (Jay's drop-off pattern repeated)** — v8 had 28s of interview before the first clipper hit; v10 faded to black at 00:41 BEFORE showing any cut. Both posts under-deliver the transformation that the interview sets up. Per `patterns/drop-offs.md → long-talk-before-cut`: cap interview at 10-12s, total ≤30s, always include the transformation reveal.
- **Open setup leakage on humor format** — v5 spent 6s on near-duplicate car shots before the saved-clients pivot at 00:09; v6 spent 6s on near-duplicate Client #1 shots before pivoting to Client #2 — same 3-second-dwell habit on the joke open. Fix: ONE setup shot max → free 3s for more payoff content. Numbered-hook posts ("3 Haircuts") need clean visual separation between the 3 clients to deliver the numeric promise.
- **Resolved (no longer coaching issues):**
  - "No verbal hook capability" — RESOLVED, 4 specialty-voiceover + 2 interview iterations confirm

hook_verbal.value: [Yari] Yo, what's up, bro? You from Allentown? [Client] I'm originally from Allentown, but I just moved to Miami. [Yari] And what makes you come over here? You go over here? [Client] Nah, nah, I flew. [Yari] Oh, shit, bro. So what makes you come to me out of all the barbers? You could've just stayed in Florida. [Client] Bro, a lot of barbers do not know how to fix this Widow's Peak. And I've been seeing on your page that you specialize in cowlicks and hairlines, so I came over here to get the Yari special, bro. [Yari] Say no more, bro. What I'm gonna do is I'm gonna give you a high taper, right, to make everything blend out even. I'm gonna cut this down, bro, because the Widow's Peak is way too dark, match it up with the sides, and give you a straight line-up. [Client] Exactly what I came here for, bro. [Yari] Let's get you ready.

hook_visual.value: Cover: face-visible side-profile of finished client (afro top with squared-off boxed lineup at temple + facial hair + freckles + light grey hoodie + gold chain visible) in pink/purple gradient lighting. In-clip open: client in glasses + KB46279 cape holding shotgun mic to mouth, Yari standing behind in white printed long-sleeve (cowboy/Western motif print) with gold rope chain + gold medallion pendant, curly hair, holding her own mic. Same Jay-import two-mic press-interview rig as v8.

hook_strength.value: 10/10 — 'Yo, what's up bro? You from Allentown?' opens innocuous, then escalates to 'I flew [from Miami]' to 'I came over here to get the Yari special' — the curiosity hook compounds into a SOCIAL-PROOF BOMBSHELL (client flew from Miami specifically) WITHIN the hook itself. The client's verbal citation of Yari's content marketing ('seeing on your page that you specialize in cowlicks and hairlines') is the strongest demand-generation evidence in the entire 10-audit 2026 sample. Caption-burned word-by-word text + dialog format keeps sound-off scrollers anchored.

hook_payoff_seconds.value: 00:17-00:28 — Yari's prescriptive 'here's what I'm gonna do' verbal payoff lands at 00:17 (high taper + Widow's Peak fix + match sides + straight line-up); client's 'Exactly what I came here for, bro' validation at 00:27. The cut transformation itself is implied to continue past frame 40 (which fades to black) — the post is structurally an INTERVIEW + PRESCRIPTION post, not interview + transformation reveal (different from v8).
