content_repeatable_format.value: Yes — SECOND iteration of the Jay-import interview format (v10 posted 2026-04-21, v8 posted 2026-04-23) — format is now confirmed as a recurring series. The mock-press-interview + client-articulated problem + Yari's prescriptive plan + Lincoln Barrow daylit studio = reusable structure for any client willing to do the on-camera interview bit. Variants by client provenance: 'flew from [city]', 'drove from [town]', 'came on a layover from [airport]', 'live across the street but heard so much about you' — the social-proof angle is the engine, the format is the vehicle.

(REPEAT LONG-FORM CLIENT COMMENTER — also commented on v6 with same supportive tone, confirmed recurring engaged fan), @king0fpressure 'Crossover episode' (peer recognizing this Miami client cut as a cross-network event). retention_predicted_dropoff.value: 00:17-00:22 — Yari's prescriptive plan ('high taper to make everything blend out even, cut this down because Widow's Peak is way too dark') is the lowest-information beat for viewers who got pulled in by the Miami-flight bombshell — they're waiting for the cut transformation that the post doesn't actually deliver (cut is implied past 00:41 fade-out, not shown). This is a structural weakness: the post sets up a transformation that it doesn't fully pay off visually.

retention_slowest_beat.value: 00:17-00:22 — Yari's prescriptive plan beat after the client's social-proof bombshell. Could be tightened to 3s max so viewers stay through the implied transformation.

verdict_what_worked.value: SECOND iteration of the Jay-import interview format + Miami-flight client + **PROVABLE DEMAND-GENERATION EVIDENCE** (client explicitly cites Yari's content marketing: 'been seeing on your page you specialize in cowlicks and hairlines, came over here to get the Yari special') + **BRANDED VERNACULAR EMERGING** ('the Yari special' — Yari now has a named signature cut in client speech) + repeat long-form client commenter (@ryansmith6543, also on v6) + cross-network peer recognition ('Crossover episode'). 185 likes / 20 comments solid mid-strong, but the content-marketing-conversion-proof is the brand-asset payoff of this post — first time the corpus has captured a client explicitly stating they booked because of the content.

verdict_what_to_change.value: Two structural fixes for the interview lane: (1) **The cover should preview the bombshell** — current side-profile cover is fine but burying the 'I FLEW FROM MIAMI' bombshell + 'Yari special' branded vernacular. Use frame 5 (mock-interview open with two-mic rig) + 'I FLEW FROM MIAMI' text overlay as cover; this is the strongest grid-tap headline this post earns. (2) **Add the transformation reveal** — post fades to black at 00:41 BEFORE showing the finished cut transformation that the client's interview set up. v8 had the transformation; v10 doesn't. Trim 10s of prescriptive plan + add 8-10s of cut + reveal so the post pays off the setup. Also worth flagging to Dack: the 'Yari special' phrase is a brand-asset Yari should claim explicitly in subsequent posts (rename a feature on her booking page, mention it in voiceover, get more clients to use the phrase on camera).

verdict_next_video_prescription.value: **RUN INTERVIEW + CUT-REVEAL hybrid (the best of v8 + v10).** Structure: 10s interview (curiosity hook + client's distance/origin + content-citation if available + client-articulated problem + Yari's 1-line prescriptive plan) → 15s cut transformation montage → 5s satisfaction beat ('Yari special done right' / 'Exactly what I came here for') → 3s 'tap in' CTA + finish frame. Total 30-35s. Always cover-frame the strongest-headline interview moment with text overlay matching that headline (this video's headline was 'I FLEW FROM MIAMI' which deserved cover billing). **'Yari special' should now be incorporated as a recurring branded phrase** — mention it in specialty-voiceover posts ('this is the Yari special — number one X specialist in Allentown') and get clients to repeat it on camera. Cross-reference patterns/structures.md → problem_agitate_solve (interview + distance-social-proof variant) and patterns/hooks.md → question (curiosity-interview + bombshell-payoff variant). Cross-reference patterns/drop-offs.md → long-talk-before-cut for tightening. **Yari-special vernacular is a top-priority brand-asset claim — flag for Dack approval to formalize into proven_scripts.**

hook_verbal.value: My boy drove an hour and 30 minutes from Hazleton just to get a hairline. Every barber he went to didn't know how to do a front taper. But he knew exactly who to go to. The number one hairline specialist in Allentown. I start laying everything down and you know what I'ma do. I'ma get that hairline crispy every trip. My boy came in looking stressed and he left knowing that he went to the best. If you need the same service, click, link, and buy the book. hook_verbal.value: My client said that there's no way that he could get a fire front taper. But y'all know I had to prove him wrong. The thing that y'all don't get is that y'all the problem and I'm the solution. And y'all know my trimmer's gonna hit every time. And this is a natural lineup too, no enhancements. If y'all tired of barber pinning on y'all hairline, y'all know who to come to. If you're in need of the same service, click, look, and buy the book.

Then I started by making sure he got the crispiest lineup. They don't call me the hairline specialist for no reason. I started chopping all his hair off from his front taper. My boy had a bush on his head. I grabbed my sharpest trimmers, and I started lining my boy up. And one thing y'all barbers don't do is take your time when y'all doing the lineup. But my client don't gotta worry about that over here. He know I'm gonna get him right. My client requested enhancements so you know we have to put that spray on there.

# Yari — Simple Audit (C0P0u40Ps9y)

_Posted 2023-11-29 · Audited 2026-05-17 · [Watch the video](https://www.instagram.com/yaricutz/reel/C0P0u40Ps9y/)_

**Bottom line:** This one worked well — repeat what it did.

## 1. Stop doing this
- Add a small persistent on-screen text label from frame 1 (e.g. 'locs + lineup') so this skill-heavy + community-flex showcase still lands when the trending audio dies or is muted, and tighten the first two seconds onto the most visually striking moment instead of the slow ring-light setup.

## 2. Keep doing this
- Riding a trending audio with a clean, aesthetically ring-light-framed 'satisfying service' showcase — the format that drove this to ~57K likes, by far Yari's top performer in the audited sample.

## 3. How the video starts (first 1–3 seconds)
- How it starts: in the middle of the action
- First words: Nothing spoken — visual only.
- Text on screen at the start: None.
- How strong the opening is: 6/10 — the ring-light framing is aesthetically distinctive and the trending audio carries it (it went Reach-focused), but there is no visual or text information hook; structurally it depends entirely on vibe and sound.
- When the viewer gets what was promised: 00:05 — clean edged-up hairline and twists revealed; full clean profile look by ~00:07

## 4. How the video flows
- Structure: show off your work
- What happens, in order: 00:00 Ring-light framed shot, client mid-service; 00:03 Pull back, storefront window reflection, service continues; 00:05 Clean edge-up reveal with vapor/steam aesthetic; 00:07 Profile beauty shots of finished lineup and twists; 00:09 Final hold on the finished look
- Speed: medium speed (2–5 sec per shot)
- Does it loop nicely? Yes — at ~9 seconds with a music bed and similar ring-light profile framing at the start and end, it loops near-seamlessly, which favors replay watch-time.

## 5. What you said
- How many words spoken: 0
- How fast you spoke: 0.0 words per second
- Your tone: calm, just showing the work

## 6. Text on the video
- How many text moments: 0
- What the text said: None.
- Caption style: Not recorded.

## 7. Where viewers might leave
- Most likely spot viewers leave: 00:00-00:02 — atmospheric ring-light framing with no immediate payoff or text; viewers not hooked by the trending audio may swipe before the clean reveal at ~00:05.
- Slowest moment: 00:00-00:03 — the through-the-ring-light setup shots carry mood but little new information until the reveal begins ~00:05.
- Off-topic moments: None.
- How spicy / debate-starting: 0/10 — purely a satisfying showcase set to music with no take or controversy.

## 8. Speed and length
- Total length: 9s
- How fast it feels: medium speed (2–5 sec per shot)
- How fast you spoke: 0.0 words per second

## 9. The ask (what you told viewers to do)
- Type of ask: Not recorded.
- Exact wording: No spoken or explicit ask.
- Asked viewers to comment? No — there is no comment prompt; the video relies on the trending audio and satisfying visuals rather than an explicit engagement ask.
- Asked viewers to save? No
- Asked viewers to share? No

## 10. How it looks (camera + setting)
- How it's shot: Shot framed through a black ring light onto the client's profile in the barber chair mid-service, with the storefront window and reflected street/parked cars behind; warm interior, no text overlay.
- Setting: Storefront barbershop shot through a ring light; large street-facing windows with parked cars and reflected 'DYNASTY' window signage; daylight, urban, polished vibe.
- People on camera: 3
- Products / brands visible: ring light; black trimmer/clippers; blue latex gloves; barber cape (Jordan Jumpman / Spider-Man print)

## 11. The cover image (what shows on the grid)
- What the cover shows: Daylight profile beauty shot of the finished locs and crisp edge-up through the storefront window (cars, reflected 'DYNASTY' signage), client in a barber-pro cape — the signature ring-light showcase aesthetic, no text.
- How strong the cover is: 7/10 — clean, on-brand aesthetic profile that suits the trending-audio showcase, but a static eyes-forward portrait with no text or motion cue undersells the satisfying clean-up payoff that drove this to ~57K.
- Does it show the finished cut? the finished cut
